title: ElectricalConnector
description: ElectricalConnector: Electrical Connectors are the devices (motors, disconnects/isolators) that serve as a source of power for mechanical equipment

MotorHorsePower.description: Motor Horsepower indicates the power required for the electrical connector without any power losses

BrakeHorsePower.description: Brake Horsepower indicates the power required for the electrical connector including any power losses

FullLoadAmps.description: Full Load Amps indicates the amount of current drawn by the motor when running, and is used for sizing the conductors

MaximumOvercurrentProtection.description: Maximum Overcurrent Protection indicates the maximum size of the fuse or breaker

Phase.description: Phase denotes the distribution of alternating current (example: single or three phase)

Frequency.description: Frequency is the rate of oscillation

Voltage.description: Voltage denotes the electrical potential difference (120/208, 277/480 (US), 240, 415 (UK)

EmergencyPower.description: Emergency Power is a boolean value (true/false) that denotes whether a device requires backup from an emergency power source

StandBy.description: Optional Standby is a boolean value (true/false) that denotes whether a device requires backup from a secondary power source, for non-critical systems

_t.description: Optional Type discirminator for BHoM type.

_bhomVersion.description: Optional Version of BHoM used when generating the object.
